mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of months with falling rates.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances or the signal loses predictive power with a t-statistic below 1.5 during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth occurs in more than 40% of months but forward returns are negative in more than 55% of those cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during tightening cycles, or 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation with Rank IC below 0.02 when industrial production rises.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding employment and rising aggregate income, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive payroll growth coincides with negative 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Year-over-year consumer price index growth exceeding 2.0 percent triggers expectations of monetary tightening and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of months where inflation exceeds the 2.0 percent threshold, or the signal fails to predict direction during high-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in industrial production and real GDP confirms synchronized supply-side and macroeconomic growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint growth condition occurs frequently but forward returns are negative in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in advance retail sales estimates signal weakening consumer demand and reduced corporate revenue expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power during easing cycles, or 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of months with declining retail sales, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.
